title: Allied remote mission support without agreement or TTCP controls
description: US engineers think no hardware movement means low friction, but the narrative is really defense-service style support with no scoped controls.

transaction_input.item_or_data_summary: Mission-system configuration notes and troubleshooting package
transaction_input.end_use_summary: Remote troubleshooting and mission planning support for allied air platform sustainment
transaction_input.access_context: Remote access session and screen share from U.S. engineers into customer environment
transaction_input.notes: Customer wants support this week. No one has cited a TAA, MLA, or TTCP.

title: Allied scoped support with named TAA and TTCP controls
description: This is the clean version of defense support. The narrative still sounds sensitive, but the control package is explicit.

transaction_input.end_use_summary: Remote troubleshooting support under existing allied sustainment agreement
transaction_input.access_context: Remote support session with named engineers only
transaction_input.notes: TAA 22-1045 covers this scope. TTCP in place. Proviso accepted and agreement scope confirmed.

title: Foreign-person source-code access without TTCP or named access list
description: The shipment story looks manageable, but the real risk is remote technical-data access by a foreign national.

transaction_input.item_or_data_summary: Flight-control source code and tuning notes
transaction_input.end_use_summary: Temporary software support for avionics sustainment
transaction_input.access_context: VPN source code access and remote repository support
transaction_input.notes: Program office wants quick access for one engineer. No TTCP, no named-person authorization memo.

title: Foreign-person access with named TTCP and bounded repo scope
description: The foreign-person risk is real, but the scope is bounded tightly enough that AI should not add noise.

transaction_input.item_or_data_summary: Flight-control source code and tuning notes
transaction_input.end_use_summary: Temporary software support for avionics sustainment
transaction_input.access_context: VPN source code access limited to named engineers only
transaction_input.notes: Named engineers only. TTCP in place. Technology Control Plan approved. Access limited to the repository branch in scope.

title: Customer scope creep turns installation into defense-style service support
description: The original install looked bounded. The new request crosses into training, troubleshooting, and new affiliate participation.

transaction_input.end_use_summary: Initial installation now expanded to operator training and mission planning assistance
transaction_input.access_context: Field service representative support plus remote follow-on sessions
transaction_input.notes: Customer expanded scope after award. New affiliate engineers may join. Proviso coverage is unclear.

title: Civil allied support with no controlled release or foreign-person access
description: This is the false-positive guardrail. Support language alone should not turn a routine civil case into an export panic.

transaction_input.end_use_summary: Direct delivery for civil maintenance evaluation
transaction_input.access_context: No remote repository, no foreign-person access, direct end user only
transaction_input.notes: Direct delivery. No broker. No onward transfer. No controlled technical-data release.

title: Prohibited foreign-person access remains a hard stop
description: The AI layer should never make this gentler than the deterministic floor.

transaction_input.item_or_data_summary: EW source code and integration notes
transaction_input.access_context: Remote source code access from inside the United States
transaction_input.notes: Customer asks whether a TCP could solve this.
